TelnetSubOptionQualifiers.brief: Constants representing Telnet suboption qualifiers used in negotiation.
TelnetSubOptionQualifiers.details: The TelnetSubOptionQualifiers group defines integer constants that specify the qualifiers used during Telnet suboption negotiations. These qualifiers determine the nature of the suboption, such as whether the option is being set or requested. The group includes 'TELQUAL_IS', indicating the current state or value of an option, and 'TELQUAL_SEND', used to request or initiate sending of an option. These constants facilitate clear and consistent handling of Telnet suboption negotiations within the system.
TelnetSubOptionQualifiers.name: TelnetSubOptionQualifiers

TELQUAL_IS.brief: Represents the 'IS' qualifier used in Telnet suboption negotiations.
TELQUAL_IS.details: This constant defines the qualifier value 'TELQUAL_IS' used within Telnet suboption negotiations to indicate that the option is being set or reported as 'is'. It is initialized to zero and is used in conjunction with other qualifiers to specify the state or mode of a Telnet option during negotiation phases. Its usage is consistent across various suboption contexts where the 'IS' state needs to be explicitly indicated.
TELQUAL_IS.name: TELQUAL_IS

TELQUAL_SEND.brief: Represents the 'SEND' sub-option qualifier used in Telnet negotiations.
TELQUAL_SEND.details: This constant defines the 'SEND' sub-option qualifier within the Telnet protocol, indicating a request to send specific options or data. It is initialized with the value 1 and is used in conjunction with other sub-option qualifiers to specify the desired action during Telnet option negotiations. The constant helps improve code readability and maintainability by replacing magic numbers with a descriptive identifier.
TELQUAL_SEND.name: TELQUAL_SEND
